---
title: "Lecture 2: Git and GitHub"
date: 2025-06-26
summary: "Intro to Git and GitHub from RStudio"
url_slides: 02-slides.qmd
url_code: csp-materials/git
format: html
page-layout: full
draft: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## Info and Materials

* **Date**: {{< meta date >}}
* **Topics**: {{< meta summary >}}
* **Slides**: [View Slides]({{< meta url_slides >}})
* **Code**: `usethis::use_course("{{< meta url_code >}}")`


## Learning Objectives

- Understand what Git is and why it's useful
- Create a Git repository in RStudio
- Connect your Git repository to GitHub
- Practice staging, committing, and pushing changes


## Before Class

- Complete Lecture 1 "Before Class"
- [Setup your computer](https://example.com/setup). Warning: this will require some time and patience, plan ahead!
- Optional: Complete the [Git + RStudio tutorial](/setup/git/git-with-rstudio)


## Readings

Skim the readings — especially the required ones — before or after class to get the big picture and prep for quizzes or assignments:

- [Happy Git and GitHub for the useR](https://happygitwithr.com/)
-
